Class Session

java.lang.Object
com.focused.core.Session
public class Session extends Object
Manage the user focus session. This is the heart of the app — pure logic, zero dependencies on JavaFX, JNA, or Windows. It can be tested without running the app. A Session knows: - which window the user wants to focus on - how long the session lasts - what state it's in (IDLE, ACTIVE, DONE)

  • Constructor Details

    • Session

      public Session(String targetWindow, int durationSeconds)
      Parameters:
      targetWindow - the window title to lock the user to
      durationSeconds - how long the session should run

  • Method Details

    • start

      public void start()
      Move from IDLE → ACTIVE. Throws if already active.

    • tick

      public boolean tick()
      Called once per second by the timer in the upper layer. Returns true if the session just finished naturally.

    • stop

      public void stop()
      Force-stop the session before the timer runs out.

    • getTargetWindow

      public String getTargetWindow()

    • getDurationSeconds

      public int getDurationSeconds()

    • getSecondsLeft

      public int getSecondsLeft()

    • getState

      public Session.State getState()

    • isActive

      public boolean isActive()

    • getProgress

      public double getProgress()
      Progress from 0.0 (just started) to 1.0 (done). Useful for the UI progress ring.
